title: Split Screen UI with Spec Panel
prompt:
# Phase 7 Task 1: Split Screen UI with Spec Panel ## CONTEXT Phase 7 adds a Design Assistant mode. The first step is creating the UI layout: a split screen with the 3D viewport on the left and an editable spec panel on the right. ## LAYOUT ``` +--------------------------------------------------+ | Toolbar | +------------------------+-------------------------+ | | Spec Panel | | 3D Viewport | - Header: "Design Spec"| | (Three.js) | - Editable textarea | | | - Buttons: Load, Save, | | | Build | +------------------------+-------------------------+ | Chat Panel | +--------------------------------------------------+ ``` ## REQUIREMENTS ### 1. HTML Structure (index.html) - Wrap viewport in a new container: `
` - Add spec panel as sibling to viewport: `
` - Spec panel contains: - Header: `
Design Spec
` - Textarea: `` - Button bar: `
` - `` - `` - `` - Add vertical resize handle between viewport and spec panel ### 2. CSS Styling (styles.css) - `#main-content`: flexbox row, contains viewport and spec panel - `#viewport`: flex: 1 (takes remaining space) - `#spec-panel`: width: 350px (fixed initially), can be resized - Spec panel hidden by default: `display: none` - When visible: `display: flex; flex-direction: column` - Textarea fills available height: `flex: 1` - Dark theme styling matching existing UI: - Background: #252526 - Border: 1px solid #3c3c3c - Textarea: monospace font, dark background (#1e1e1e) - Vertical resize handle: 4px wide, cursor: col-resize ### 3. Resize Handle (renderer.js) - Draggable divider between viewport and spec panel - Min spec panel width: 250px - Max spec panel width: 50% of window - Similar pattern to existing chat panel resize ### 4. Initial State - Spec panel hidden on app start - Will be shown when design mode activates (next task) ## FILES TO MODIFY - src/renderer/index.html - src/renderer/styles.css - src/renderer/renderer.js (resize logic) ## TESTING 1. npm start 2. Manually show spec panel: In dev console, run `document.getElementById('spec-panel').style.display = 'flex'` 3. Verify layout: viewport on left, spec panel on right 4. Verify textarea is editable 5. Verify resize handle works 6. Verify buttons are visible (non-functional for now) ## DO NOT - Implement button functionality yet (next tasks) - Implement design mode toggle yet (next task) - Connect to Claude yet Provide a plan before implementing.
